input.title: Copying in Range-Based For Loop

output.technical: Using `auto item` creates a deep copy of the string for each iteration of the loop. Using `const auto& item` binds a reference to the element, preventing the copy.
output.beginner: You are making a brand new copy of every word before printing it. It's much faster to just look at the original word.
output.analogy: It's like photocopying a document just so you can read it, then throwing the copy away.
output.prevention: Always use `const auto&` in range-based for loops unless you need to mutate a copy.

output.analogy: It's like photocopying a document just so you can read it, then throwing the copy away.
output.prevention: Always use `const auto&` in range-based for loops unless you need to mutate a copy.
